title: Today's Tech Brief: PlayStation State of Play June 2026: All the news and trailers
summary: A rundown of the latest tech news, including PlayStation's State of Play and Google's new scam detection feature.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for PlayStation State of Play June 2026: All the news and trailers
imageCaption: PlayStation State of Play June 2026: All the news and trailers (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. We're kicking off with some exciting news from the world of gaming and tech. Sony recently hosted its PlayStation State of Play event, and I've got the lowdown on what was announced. Meanwhile, Google is stepping up its game to protect users from scams. And, as a bonus, I'll share a quick thought on why these updates caught my attention.

First up, PlayStation's State of Play event took place in June 2026. According to The Verge, the event was a chance for Sony to showcase its upcoming games and give fans a glimpse into what's next for the platform. I'm looking at the article from The Verge, which provides a comprehensive rundown of all the news and trailers that came out of the event. What I find interesting is that this event is a great example of how gaming companies are working hard to keep fans engaged and excited about what's to come.

Next, Google is making it easier for users to avoid scams. The company's Phone app will now flag suspicious calls that may be impersonating one of your contacts. This feature uses AI to detect potential scams and alert users, giving them the option to hang up. I think this is a great example of how technology can be used to make our lives easier and safer. We've all received those annoying scam calls, so it's nice to see Google taking steps to help us avoid them.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
